title: Exercises: Future Rounds and Ongoing Standardization

exercises.title: Round 4 KEM Comparison
exercises.description: Compare the three Round 4 KEM candidates to determine which might be standardized.
tasks: Create a comparison table for BIKE, HQC, and Classic McEliece
Include: key sizes, ciphertext sizes, performance, cryptanalysis history
Analyze the tradeoffs of each for different use cases
Predict which is most likely to be standardized and why
Identify scenarios where Classic McEliece's huge keys are acceptable
exercises.expectedOutput: A detailed comparison document with predictions and analysis
hints: McEliece has smallest ciphertext but largest keys
BIKE and HQC have similar size profiles
Consider long-term storage vs network transmission scenarios

exercises.title: Crypto-Agility Architecture Design
exercises.description: Design a crypto-agile system that can adapt to future PQC standards.
tasks: Define abstract interfaces for KEM and signature operations
Design a configuration system for algorithm selection
Plan for parameter set updates within the same algorithm
Create a migration strategy for adding new algorithms
Document how to handle algorithm deprecation
exercises.expectedOutput: An architectural design document with interfaces and migration procedures
hints: Use algorithm identifiers like those in JOSE/COSE
Consider both software and HSM-based implementations
Plan for testing new algorithms before production deployment

exercises.title: FALCON Implementation Challenges
exercises.description: Research the implementation challenges that delayed FALCON/FN-DSA standardization.
tasks: Explain why floating-point arithmetic is problematic for cryptography
Describe how Gaussian sampling can leak information
Research fixed-point alternatives proposed for FALCON
Compare implementation complexity to ML-DSA
Propose criteria for when FN-DSA should be preferred despite complexity
exercises.expectedOutput: A technical analysis document on FALCON implementation challenges
hints: Floating-point results can vary across platforms
Timing variations in rejection sampling leak information
Look at academic papers on constant-time FALCON

exercises.title: PQC Portfolio Planning
exercises.description: Create a PQC algorithm portfolio plan for a specific organization type.
tasks: Choose an organization type (e.g., bank, hospital, government agency)
Identify all cryptographic use cases in that organization
Map current algorithms to appropriate PQC replacements
Include timeline for adoption of current and future standards
Plan for algorithm diversity as additional standards become available
exercises.expectedOutput: A comprehensive portfolio plan with adoption timeline
hints: Consider which future Round 4 KEMs might benefit your use cases
